programme.title: Live UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Paris Saint-Germain v Arsenal (Kick-off 5.00pm). Coverage of the final from Puskas Arena in Budapest. The Gunners made it to their first final in this competition since 2006 thanks to a 2-1 semi-final victory over Atletico Madrid, with Bukayo Saka scoring the winner in the second leg at the Emirates. PSG beat Bayern Munich 6-5 across two epic encounters to reach their second successive Champions League final. The French club are looking to become the first back-to-back winners of the competition since Real Madrid won three titles in a row between 2016 and 2018.

Brighton reached this match thanks to a 3-2 comeback victory over Liverpool in the semi-finals after they had gone 2-0 down inside 22 minutes. The other semi-final saw a very similar outcome as Manchester City came from two goals down against Chelsea to take the game to extra-time, before Khadija Shaw scored a 103rd-minute winner to send City through to the final.

programme.desc: Coverage from around the courts on day seven of the Grand Slam tournament at Stade Roland Garros in Paris, featuring third-round matches. Spain's Carlos Alcaraz will be looking to join the likes of Novak Djokovic, Ivan Lendl and Gustavo Kuerten in winning this event three times, having previously won here in 2024 and 2025. In the women's singles tournament, last year's winner Coco Gauff will join players such as Maria Sharapova and Martina Navratilova in lifting the trophy here on two seperate occasions.

At this stage last year in the men's singles event, eventual runner-up Jannik Sinner beat Andrey Rublev 6-1, 6-3, 6-4 to progress, while 2025 winner Carlos Alcaraz beat Benjamin Shelton 7-6, 6-3, 4-6, 6-4. programme.desc: Los Angeles Dodgers v Philadelphia Phillies (Start-time 9.10pm). Coverage of the National League match from Dodger Stadium. These two sides last met in the National League Divisional Series back in early October, a series that saw the Dodgers progress thanks to a 3-1 series victory. Los Angeles won the first two games 5-3 and 4-3 in Philadelphia, before the Phillies won game three 8-2. The hosts then won the fourth contest 2-1 to move onto a Championship series against Milwaukee Brewers.

programme.desc: Coverage from around the courts on day nine of the Grand Slam tournament at Stade Roland Garros in Paris, featuring fourth-round matches. America's Coco Gauff won the women's singles title a year ago thanks to a 7-6, 2-6, 4-6 victory over Belarus' Aryna Sabalenka in the final. Gauff beat the likes of Lois Boisson, Ekaterina Alexandrova and fellow American Madison Keys to reach the showpiece, while Sabalenka beat Iga Swiatek, Elina Svitolina and Amanda Anisimova to set up the clash with Gauff.

programme.title: 2007 Djokovic v Federer
programme.desc: At the 2007 Canadian Open, Novak Djokovic defeated the world's top three players in successive matches en route to his second Masters title, including victory in the final over Federer.

programme.title: 2009 Djokovic v Monfils
programme.desc: In one of the most dramatic finals Bercy has ever seen, Novak Djokovic and Gael Monfils went toe to toe in a tightly fought, three-set battle for the 2009 Rolex Paris Masters title.

programme.title: 2011 Djokovic v Nadal
programme.desc: In the 2011 Madrid Open final at the Caja Magica, Novak Djokovic inflicted the first clay-court defeat on Rafael Nadal in nearly two years to claim his fifth title of the year.

programme.title: 2011 Djokovic v Nadal
programme.desc: During a year in which Novak Djokovic dominated Rafael Nadal, one of the Serb's most high-profile victories over the legendary Spaniard came in the final of the 2011 US Open.

programme.title: 2012 Djokovic v Murray
programme.desc: After saving five championship points, Novak Djokovic roared back to dethrone the two-time reigning Shanghai Masters champion Andy Murray in an epic, three-set 2012 thriller.

programme.title: 2012 Djokovic v Federer
programme.desc: With the 2012 season-ending title on the line at the O2 Arena, two-time defending champion Roger Federer looked to deny world number one Novak Djokovic a second ATP world title.

programme.title: 2013 Djokovic v Nadal
programme.desc: In the 2013 final of the Monte-Carlo Rolex Masters, Novak Djokovic brought an end to Rafael Nadal's eight-year reign as champion in the Principality.

programme.title: 2014 Djokovic v Federer
programme.desc: Fighting back to beat the great Roger Federer, a 26-year-old Novak Djokovic clinched his third Indian Wells title in a riveting 2014 final that went all the way to a third-set tie-break.

programme.title: 2023 Djokovic v Alcaraz
programme.desc: Out to avenge the five-set Wimbledon final defeat inflicted by the Spaniard, Novak Djokovic finally overcame Carlos Alcaraz in just under four hours in the 2023 Cincinnati final.

programme.title: 2023 Djokovic v Medvedev
programme.desc: In a repeat of the 2021 final, the winner on that day, Daniil Medvedev, fought three-time champion Novak Djokovic in the final of the 2023 men's singles at the US Open.

programme.title: 2025 Djokovic v Hurkacz
programme.desc: In what was a chance for Novak Djokovic to join Jimmy Connors and Roger Federer in winning a 100th tour-level title, the 38-year-old Serb battled Hubert Hurkacz in the Geneva Open final.
